seo.title: Prompt Injection Prevention for AI Agents in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix prompt injection prevention for ai agents in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Semantic Kernel agents, prompt injection manifests as malicious inputs exploiting the Kernel's plugin chaining, planners, and memory systems, bypassing safeguards during function invocation or plan generation. Attackers craft inputs that hijack the Handlebars-like prompt templates or manipulate the sequential plugin execution flow. This leads to unauthorized function calls, data exfiltration through memory recall, or cascading failures across the agent orchestration layer.

howItManifests.symptom: Malicious input triggers unintended plugin sequence via injected function calls
howItManifests.impact: Cascading errors execute unauthorized actions across multiple plugins

howItManifests.symptom: Injected instructions override HandleRequestAsync planning logic
howItManifests.impact: Generates invalid execution plans that bypass security controls

howItManifests.symptom: Fake conversation history injected into SemanticTextMemory
howItManifests.impact: Hallucinates past interactions leading to privilege escalation

howItManifests.symptom: Injected payloads propagate between isolated plugins
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive data contamination across plugin boundaries

detection.name: Plugin Function Call Injection Detector
detection.description: Scans function arguments for suspicious Semantic Kernel function invocation patterns in user input

detection.name: Planner Prompt Override Detector
detection.description: Monitors ActionPlanner inputs for Handlebars template injection attempts

detection.name: Memory Recall Tampering Detector
detection.description: Validates SemanticTextMemory recall inputs against known conversation structure

fixes.name: Kernel Function Invocation Whitelist
fixes.description: Restrict plugin function calls to pre-approved functions using KernelFunctionFilter
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel

class FunctionWhitelistFilter:
    def __init__(self, allowed_functions: set):
        self.allowed_functions = allowed_functions
    
    async def invoke_filter(self, function_args, kernel):
        calling_function = function_args.get('function_name')
        if calling_function not in self.allowed_functions:
            raise ValueError(f'Unauthorized function: {calling_function}')
        return function_args

# Usage
kernel = Kernel()
whitelist_filter = FunctionWhitelistFilter({'approvedPlugin.calculate', 'math.add'})
kernel.register_function_invocation_filter(whitelist_filter.invoke_filter)

fixes.name: Planner Input Sanitizer
fixes.description: Sanitize ActionPlanner inputs using content filtering before plan generation
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel.planning import ActionPlanner

import re

def sanitize_planner_input(input_text: str) -> str:
    # Remove dangerous Handlebars and function patterns
    dangerous_patterns = [
        r'\{\{.*?\}\}',
        r'invoke\s*\(.*?\)',
        r'getPlugin\s*\(.*?\)',
        r'runAsync\s*\(.*?\)'
    ]
    
    sanitized = input_text
    for pattern in dangerous_patterns:
        sanitized = re.sub(pattern, '[SANITIZED]', sanitized, flags=re.DOTALL)
    return sanitized

# Usage with planner
planner = ActionPlanner(kernel)
plan = await planner.create_plan(sanitize_planner_input(user_input))

fixes.name: Memory Input Validator
fixes.description: Validate all SemanticTextMemory inputs against conversation schema

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Prompt Injection Prevention for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a Semantic Kernel filter on function results, automatically scanning all plugin outputs, planner decisions, and memory recalls for injection artifacts. It monitors the entire agent execution flow - from user input processing through plugin chaining to final response generation - catching sophisticated attacks that span multiple turns. Vex's ML-powered detectors recognize Semantic Kernel-specific injection patterns including Handlebars template abuse, function invocation smuggling, and memory contamination attempts that traditional string matching misses.

The Vex SDK (`pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk`) provides drop-in KernelFunctionResultFilters that wrap your existing agent pipelines without code changes. Vex automatically generates remediation plans, such as quarantining malicious plugin chains or rewriting compromised plans, and provides detailed attack forensics showing exactly which Kernel component was exploited. Integration takes 3 lines of code and catches 95% of known Semantic Kernel injection vectors out-of-the-box.
vexAutomation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from vex_sdk import VexKernelFilter

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk

kernel = Kernel()

# 3-line Vex integration
vex_filter = VexKernelFilter(
    detectors=['prompt-injection', 'sk-planner-hijack'],
    auto_remediate=True
)

kernel.register_function_result_filter(vex_filter.filter_function_results)

# Your existing agent code continues unchanged
chat_service = kernel.get_chat_service('gpt-4')
result = await chat_service.get_chat_message_contents(
    chat_history, settings
)

# Vex automatically:
# ✅ Blocks injected function calls
# ✅ Sanitizes planner outputs
# ✅ Validates memory recalls
# ✅ Logs attack forensics
